issue_title: Realm import fails when client configures default_acr values
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

Importing an exported json realm configuration fails if it contains a custom acr_to_loa mapping and a client that references the acr value via default_acr_values.

### Version

Keycloak 25.0.5

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Import should work fine.

This issue is present in KC25 and KC24

### Actual behavior

Import fails with an exception:


```
2024-09-11 12:33:48,828 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-19) Uncaught server error: java.lang.RuntimeException: Invalid client client-silver: Default ACR values need to contain values specified in the ACR-To-Loa mapping or number levels from set realm browser flow
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.lambda$createClients$7(DefaultExportImportManager.java:541)
	at org.keycloak.validation.ValidationUtil.validateClient(ValidationUtil.java:45)
	at org.keycloak.validation.ValidationUtil.validateClient(ValidationUtil.java:28)
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.createClients(DefaultExportImportManager.java:540)
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.importRealm(DefaultExportImportManager.java:369)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.RepresentationToModel.importRealm(RepresentationToModel.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.RealmManager.importRealm(RealmManager.java:584)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.RealmManager.importRealm(RealmManager.java:513)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.RealmManagerProviderFactory.lambda$postInit$0(RealmManagerProviderFactory.java:54)
	at org.keycloak.services.DefaultKeycloakSessionFactory.publish(DefaultKeycloakSessionFactory.java:91)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ImportRealmFromRepresentationEvent.fire(ImportRealmFromRepresentationEvent.java:50)
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.importRealm(DefaultExportImportManager.java:172)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmsAdminResource.importRealm(RealmsAdminResource.java:147)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmsAdminResource$quarkusrestinvoker$importRealm_aa9defcbd5e1fb410aecd3ab57b280946687671e.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:635)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2516)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2495)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1521)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:11)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:11)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)

```

### How to Reproduce?

1) Create a realm
2) Create a acr-to-loa mapping in the realm, e.g. silver: 1 and gold: 2
3) Create a client
4) In advanced client settings configure "Default ACR Values to silver"
5) Export the realm
6) Delete the realm
7) Try to reimport the realm json

### Anything else?

The problem seems to be that the realm attributes are not yet set in org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager#importRealm(org.keycloak.representations.idm.RealmRepresentation, org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, boolean)
when the client configuration is imported. However the `acr.loa.map` realm attribute is needed to validate the client configuration.

I propose to move the realm attribute import above the client import.
This solves the issue.

An example file that reproduces the error can be found here: https://gist.github.com/thomasdarimont/b0928cf6d6df07dc76d52413794a1414
commit_message:
Ensure realm attributes import happens before client import

Fixes #32799

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Requested `grant_types` inconsistent with created `grant_types` for OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When specifying `grant_types` in a OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration the requested and returned `grant_types` do not match. 

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Requested and returned `grant_types` are the same

### Actual behavior

Requested `grant_types` results in different created `grant-types`:

* `authorization_code` --> `authorization_code` [OK]
* `client_credentials` --> `[authorization_code, client_credentials]` [NOT OK]
* `password` --> `authorization_code` [NOT OK]
* `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code` --> `authorization_code` [NOT OK]

### How to Reproduce?

Use `kct` to dynamically create clients for different flows, using the following command replacing flows with browser, client, password, and device, to try the different grant types:

```
kct config context create --iss myissuer -c mycontext --create-client --flow <FLOW> -X
```

Before running the above you need to create an issuer in `kct`, and context with a flow that uses boostrap admin user or client, and register that context with the issuer as the `client-registration-context`.

Using `-X` option you will see the request sent to Keycloak, and the responses.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Requested grant_types inconsistent with created grant_types for OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration
closes #32801

issue_title: Lightweight access token is not working for bootstrap admin client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

I tested out https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32347 with the bootstrap admin client, and ran into some issues: 

* Bootstrap admin client doesn't use lightweight tokens, which it should by default
* When enabling `Always use lightweight access token` for the client I get a 403

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Bootstrap admin client uses lightweight access token by default, and works out of the box

### Actual behavior

Uses regular tokens by default, and results in a 403 if `Always use lightweight access token` is enabled for the client. I suspect this is down to the fact that in `https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32347` it states it uses the presence of the `sub` claim to determine if it's a lightweight access token or not, but the token I get with `Always use lightweight access token` includes the sub claim:

```
$ kct token -c admin -d
{
  "exp" : 1726053554,
  "iat" : 1726053494,
  "jti" : "286867a7-e822-4a2c-b3e8-4355d917117a",
  "iss" : "http://localhost:8080/realms/master",
  "sub" : "eb7bfd3a-bb09-477f-94d9-e0fa2ff0ee05",
  "typ" : "Bearer",
  "azp" : "temp-admin",
  "scope" : "profile email"
}
```

### How to Reproduce?

Start Keycloak with `--bootstrap-admin-client-secret=mysecret` and grab a token for that client, decode it and see that it contains all claims. Enable `Always use lightweight access token` for the client, and try to invoke the Admin REST APIs with it, and you'll get a 403.

Another thing is that it seems the `sub` claim should not be present in a lightweight access token by default, but it still is:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df7be101-0d21-4563-b209-fe0b6ab6b444)


### Anything else?

_No response_

This was not catched by the testsuite as we don't have any test application deploying keys in the PEM format. Just quickstart uses it, so it was catched by the quickstart: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-quickstarts/issues/595

nightly (from 2024-09-11)

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Actual behavior

Application fails to deploy to Wildfly 30 with the issue mentioned in the description

### How to Reproduce?

- Download Wildfly 30
- install SAML adapter via galleon feature pack to it from the latest Keycloak main
- Deploy the application with keycloak-saml.xml having the keys in PEM format

NOTE: This was reproduced with the SAML quickstart (Related issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-quickstarts/issues/595 )



### Anything else?

issue_title: Login V2 theme: Pages specify fewer tabindex entries
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

When looking in the base login.ftl, this specifies 8 tabindex attributes. When looking at the Login v2 login.ftl, it specifies only one random tabindex (6). 

This is an accessibility issue. 

### Version

main

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Tab key should cycle through the items in the order they are displayed in the browser.

### Actual behavior

Fewer tabindex attributes are set up. This might lead to items appear in the wrong order when using "tab". 

In this case, the "forgot password" link is not reachable in the natural order. On Chrome it is available only after iterating through all items in the form, the address bar, the bookmark item, and only then it is in focus. 

### How to Reproduce?

Review the code

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: TOTP QR codes broken when realm display name contains colon character
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug When a user tries to register a TOTP code generator via QR code and the realm display name contains ":" characters, then the generated TOTP QR contains an invalid URI. ### Version 24.0.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior TOTP QR Codes for realms with colon character should still work. ### Actual behavior TOTP QR code cannot be registered in any authenticator app because the QR code contains a broken URL. ### How to Reproduce? 1) Create a realm with a display name that contains a `:` character 2) Create a user with password 3) Try to register an TOTP code generator by scanning a QR code The QR code app (e.g. freeotp) will show an error message like "invalid code". An example for a broken QR code is: The broken otpauth URI looks like: `otpauth://totp/Invalid%3ARealm:tester?secret=MZWDMVLFNJWVCVSDOMYUO6RYKNHDO3KE&digits=6&algorithm=SHA1&issuer=Invalid%3ARealm&period=30` ### Anything else? The problem is that the realm display name is used as the label for the QR code generator URL. If the realm display name contains characters that are not allowed in an URL. We should ensure that the issuer in the otpauth URL contains no invalid characters like (`:` or `/`). Suggestion replace those characters with a space (`%20`).
commit_message:
Revise KeyURI generation in OTPPolicy

- More idiomatic URLEncoder usage
- Replace `:` character with space in issuer if present in OTP label
- Ease testing for OTP KeyURI generation

Fixes #32833

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Admin UI defaults to master realm even without permissions to it
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When logging into the admin UI (`/admin/master/console/`) with a user that has no permissions to view the `master` realm, the user is greeted with a "Network response was not OK." message because `whoami?currentRealm=master` returns a 403 Forbidden.

When then selecting the correct realm in the drop down the UI does not reload/change, while the URL does (`/admin/master/console/#/test`) and clicking "Press here to refresh and continue" the default URL (`/admin/master/console/`) is refreshed, not the one of the just selected realm.

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that the UI redirects to the first valid realm and does not fallback/stop to the default `master` realm, when the user does not have access to it.

At least I would expect, that selecting a valid realm in the drop down, refreshes the UI and actually loads the content of the requested realm (not just the navigation bar on the left).

### Actual behavior

See "Describe the bug" ☝🏻 

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a user that has no access to the `master` realm. For example with a group like this:

```yml
groups:
  - name: user-manager
    clientRoles:
      test-realm:
        - view-users
        - query-users
        - manage-users
        - query-groups
```

2. Log in with that user on `<url>/admin/master/console/`
3. You are greeted with
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3234440e-8590-43c4-81f1-69b3ed4b2050)
4. Selecting "test" in the realm drop down and only updates the navigation bar on the left, not the content itself.
5. Clicking "Press here to refresh and continue" redirects back to `<url>/admin/master/console/` which results in 3.

### Anything else?

When directly calling the UI for a valid realm (`/admin/master/console/#/test`), then it works as expected. So as a workaround the users need to bookmark the "correct" URL, instead of the base URL.

issue_title: Prevent deadlocks on concurrent user updates
issue_body:
### Description

The current functionality when setting a user attribute will first remove the user attribute using a named query. 

When doing this, and two operations execute the deleted for the same user in for the attributes in a different order, this can lead to deadlocks. 

See for the current code: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/37f42430e6b99ac261d8936dbd1f41b9de9bfb3d/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/UserAdapter.java#L206-L233



### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

While the result of a concurrent update is usually between "last write wins" and a call wouldn't know if they won, we can avoid a deadlock by sorting the updates to ensure a stable ordering. 




### Details

Reproducer: 

Disable auto-commit and aetup the data:

```
insert into user_attribute (name, value, user_id, id) values ('1', 'v', 'c7332040-acfd-41a4-90cd-a61c408b040b', '1');

insert into user_attribute (name, value, user_id, id) values ('2', 'v', 'c7332040-acfd-41a4-90cd-a61c408b040b', '2');
```

Run in session one: 

```
delete from user_attribute where user_id = 'c7332040-acfd-41a4-90cd-a61c408b040b' and name = '1';
```

Run in session two:

```
delete from user_attribute where user_id = 'c7332040-acfd-41a4-90cd-a61c408b040b' and name = '2';

delete from user_attribute where user_id = 'c7332040-acfd-41a4-90cd-a61c408b040b' and name = '1';
```

This leads to a DB error:

```
[2024-09-12 12:00:31] [40P01] ERROR: deadlock detected
[2024-09-12 12:00:31] Detail: Process 234 waits for ShareLock on transaction 21200; blocked by process 232.
[2024-09-12 12:00:31] Process 232 waits for ShareLock on transaction 21201; blocked by process 234.
[2024-09-12 12:00:31] Hint: See server log for query details.
[2024-09-12 12:00:31] Where: while deleting tuple (0,5) in relation "user_attribute"
```
commit_message:
Sort order of updates for user properties (#32853)

This should reduce deadlocks on the user property table if the users are updated concurrently.

Closes #32852

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Database index creation isn’t skipped on large data sets in Keycloak 24
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area docs ### Describe the bug Hi everyone, we’re working on the migration from Keycloak 23.0.7 to 24.0.5, and we have a question about a specific change that is mentioned in the migration guide. The migration guide says: > This change adds new indexes on the tables USER_ATTRIBUTE and FED_USER_ATTRIBUTE. If those tables contain more than 300000 entries, Keycloak will skip the index creation by default during the automatic schema migration and instead log the SQL statement on the console during migration to be applied manually after Keycloak’s startup. See the [Upgrading Guide](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/25.0.5/upgrading/) for details on how to configure a different limit. source: https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/upgrading/#user-attribute-value-length-extension In our use case the `fed_user_attribute` table contains way more than 300000 entries. But it doesn’t skip the index creation. We’ve noticed that Keycloak uses the ` QuarkusLiquibaseConnectionProvider` instead of the `DefaultLiquibaseConnectionProvider` which actually contains and configures the `indexCreationThreshold` with a default value of `300000`. Moreover we’ve seen that the `QuarkusLiquibaseConnectionProvider` lives in a legacy package and we’re not sure if that class should be used at all. We’ve started a default Keycloak image with 24.0.5 and noticed that the index got created every time, no matter how many entries exist in the db table. That’s because the parameter mentioned above is not being initialized properly. What is the desired behavior regarding the Liquibase initialization process? Which provider implementation should be used? And how does that fit to the documentation? Do we miss anything? ### Version 24.0.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The index creation should be skipped and an sql statement should be printed to the console. ### Actual behavior the indexes are created automatically no matter how many entries are in the database table ### How to Reproduce? - run the `docker-compose.yml` file with keycloak version 23.0.7 - import data to the `fed_user_attribute` table and make sure to persist the data to a volume - run the `docker-compose.yml` file with keycloak version 24.0.5 ```yaml services: postgres: image: postgres:16 container_name: postgres environment: POSTGRES_DB: keycloak POSTGRES_USER: user POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password volumes: - ./postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data ports: - "5432:5432" networks: - local keycloak: # image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:23.0.7 image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:24.0.5 container_name: keycloak entrypoint: ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh", "start", "--db", "postgres"] environment: KC_DB_USERNAME: user KC_DB_PASSWORD: password KC_DB_URL_HOST: postgres KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin KC_HTTP_ENABLED: true KC_HOSTNAME_STRICT: false ports: - "8080:8080" networks: - local depends_on: - postgres networks: local: name: local driver: bridge ``` ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Make `QuarkusLiquibaseConnectionProvider` extending `DefaultLiquibaseConnectionProvider` to allow `indexCreationThreshold` working

Closes #32860

Signed-off-by: vramik <vramik@redhat.com>

issue_title: Increased DB activity due to changes in LDAPStorageManager.searchForUserByUserAttributeStream
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

After changes introduced by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/f78c54fa42c09f76870e093b5496cc99da5f0f3b the `searchForUserByUserAttributeStream` implementation in `LDAPStorageProvider` is no longer checking if the local user exists before importing the user from LDAP. This causes the users to be re-imported every time this method is called, and this triggers the execution of all LDAP attribute mappers, which in turn leads to an increase in the number of DELETE and INSERT statements performed to update user attributes.

This search method is also called when authenticating using X509 client certificates.

### Version

22.0.12 and main

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The method should still be checking whether the user exists before importing, just like all other user search methods in the provider.

### Actual behavior

Method doesn't check if the local user exists, and re-imports the users every time it is called.

### How to Reproduce?

One way to reproduce it is to authenticate using X509 client certs, and enable logging for hibernate (INFO,org.hibernate.SQL:debug,org.keycloak:debug). Then check the logs - they will have several logs for importing the users, along with many SQL statements to update the attributes.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not automatically re-import users if they already exist locally when searching by attributes

Closes #32870

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: Inconsistency of the access token iat after setting the time offset in the test suite
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When the time offset is set in the `AbstractKeycloakTest`, the token is also forced to refresh by the `TokenManager` of the `adminClient` (see [Keycloak GitHub - AbstractKeycloakTest](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/AbstractKeycloakTest.java#L698-L712)).

However, if the refresh token has expired because the new `Time.currentTime() > the refresh token expiration`, a new access token is requested using the password grant creating a new user session on the server.

When the time offset is reset at the [end of the test](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/31ed3db41343376b4c371feb8fc270382c1336eb/testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/AbstractKeycloakTest.java#L231-L233), or manually set back in the past, the token refresh is forced again with success this time because `Time.currentTime() < the refresh token expiration`. 

This causes an issue where the new access token is valid but has an `iat < userSession.getStarted()`, creating an inconsistency and making it impossible to perform an introspection or a userinfo request with this new access token.

Additionally, the check that the `access token iat < userSession.getStarted()` is not consistently performed across all Keycloak when the access token is used. For instance, this token can be used for the Admin REST API.

This issue should only occur in the test suite when the time offset is changed and should not affect the normal usage of tokens. However, it would be nice to fix this issue for the test suite and add additional checks to ensure consistency between `iat` and the user session where it is currently missing.

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The `adminClient` in the test suite should always work when the time offset is changed. The check of the `iat` and user session start time should be more consistent across Keycloak.

### Actual behavior

When the `TokenManager` of the `adminClient` refreshes the token after the time offset is reset, it causes a new access token to be issued before the user session start time. This token can be used for the Admin REST API, but it is not valid for userinfo and introspection.

### How to Reproduce?

In a test that extends `AbstractKeycloakTest`:
1. Set the time offset to 2000 using `setTimeOffset(2000)`.
2. Set the time offset back to 0 using `setTimeOffset(0)`.
3. Call an admin API endpoint using `adminClient.tokenManager().getAccessTokenString()`.
4. Assert that the response is 200.
5. Call the userinfo endpoint using `adminClient.tokenManager().getAccessTokenString()`.
6. Assert that the response is 401.
7. Call introspection using `adminClient.tokenManager().getAccessTokenString()`.
8. Assert that the response is 200 with response content `{"active": false}`.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Administrator username changed in master realm after configuring email address for SMTP connection test for another realm with "Email as username" enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

After I create a realm and toggle on in the realm settings "Email as username", when I configure the SMTP settings and want to test the connection, I am asked to setup an email address.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/099eb893-1849-40cf-ab82-343cd2559fde)

If I click the link to setup an email address, this is the admin user details form before setting it.
![admin-before-email-change](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/802dd7bc-5128-41ab-b2e0-4f999ef689cb)

After setting the email address, the username of the administrator user in the master realm is also changed to the email address we set for the test.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9142d9eb-05b8-4d88-ad76-674374f6fe76)

and we are not able to login anymore in the administration console using the old username "admin", we have to use the test email address, instead.

It is worth noting that the master realm to which the administrator user belongs does not have the "Email as username" option enabled. So it's really a surprising and confusing behavior.

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The test email should be set only as an email address and should not overwrite the username for the administrator account

### Actual behavior

The administrator account username is also set to the email address and cannot login anymore with the previous credentials

### How to Reproduce?

Tested on Ubuntu 20.04 with a freshly downloaded standalone instance of Keycloak 25.0.5

- Start the instance `bin/kc.sh start-dev`
- Browse to the Keycloak URL
- Configure an administration account (admin) with password
- Log in as administrator
- Create a new realm
- Enable the option "Email as username" in the new realm
- Configure the SMTP settings for the realm and setup an email address to be used for the test
- Log out and try logging back in as the administrator user

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Incorrect Disclosure Handling in SdJwtVP.of(String) Method
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

There is parsing error in the "SdJwtVP.java"  file when the length of 'disclosureStart' equals 'disclosureEnd'. In this case, the value should be empty, and the assignment should only occur when 'End > Start'. 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5fe916861df60a8791b14d84474eaa517ca97c81/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/sdjwt/vp/SdJwtVP.java#L104-L110

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When "disclosureStart"  equals "disclosureEnd", the value should be empty. The assignment should occur when End > Start.

### Actual behavior

An IllegalArgumentException" is thrown with the misleading error message " ("SD-JWT is malformed, expected to end with " + SdJwt.DELIMITER) ".

### How to Reproduce?

Create an SD-JWT wwithout the delimiter at the end (e.g., "issuer-signed-jwt" instead of "issuer-signed-jwt).

Attempt to parse the SD-JWT using the `SdJwtVp.of()` method.

### Anything else?

I'll send a PR to address the issue.

issue_title: RESTART_AUTHENTICATION_ERROR in Iphone devices (using safari and chrome browser)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Hello everyone,

I'm experiencing an issue with Keycloak when authenticating on iPhone devices (Safari and Chrome). The authentication flow works correctly on desktop browsers (Chrome on Windows), but on iPhone, the process fails, and instead of receiving the expected authorization code parameter, the authentication is restarted, or I receive a temporarily_unavailable error.

Working Flow (Desktop Browser - Chrome)

The user is redirected to Keycloak and logs in successfully.
After login, the browser receives a URL with the code= parameter, which is then used to complete the OpenID Connect flow and obtain tokens.
Everything works as expected.

Failing Flow (iPhone - Safari/Chrome)
The user is redirected to Keycloak and logs in successfully.
Instead of receiving the code= parameter, the authentication flow redirects to /login-actions/restart with a 302 status, indicating that the authentication session is being restarted.
This flow never completes, and an error temporarily_unavailable or authentication_expired appears in the logs.



### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

After login, the browser receives a URL with the code= parameter, which is then used to complete the OpenID Connect flow and obtain tokens.

### Actual behavior

The user is redirected to Keycloak and logs in successfully.
Instead of receiving the code= parameter, the authentication flow redirects to /login-actions/restart with a 302 status, indicating that the authentication session is being restarted.

### How to Reproduce?

have an isntance of Keycloak 25.0.1 running
create a confidential client in master realm
Enable Authentication Flow
and set a redirect Uri
Test the Flow on Desktop Browser
Test the Flow on iPhone (Safari or Chrome)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Passkeys: Infinite (re-)loading loop on browsers with WebAuthn Conditional UI disabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

### Summary
With the introduction of Webauthn Conditional UI in keycloak (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/24305) an infinite (re-)loading situation was introduced. For any Webauthn/Passkey API error which occurs after the page is loaded, the browser goes into a infinite loop.

### Analysis
Any error occurring when executing `initAuthenticate()` in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/resources/js/passkeysConditionalAuth.js#L54 is propagated to `returnFailure()`.
This then sets the error to an invisible form-field, which is then POSTed to keycloak. 

```
export function returnFailure(err) {
    document.getElementById("error").value = err;
    document.getElementById("webauth").submit();
}
```

Using this pattern of POSTing back the error causes infinite loops, especially for code paths that are directly executed again after the page has successfully loaded.

### How to fix 
Use a different pattern to show errors (not POSTing back?) or do not execute code path if error is currently displayed (from previous request).

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should not send browser into infinite (re-)loading loop if certain features are not available.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak sends browser into infinite loading loop.

### How to Reproduce?

Install DuckDuckGo browser (for example on macOS or Android) or any other browser that does not yet support Passkey Conditional UI but already exposes `PublicKeyCredential.isConditionalMediationAvailable` as a function. 

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix: ignore errors caused by webauthn's conditional ui to avoid loop

closes #33072

Signed-off-by: Fabian Aggeler <aggeler@ubique.ch>

issue_title: The "Valid redirect URIs" field is not displayed when the "Standard flow" is unchecked
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Apache Guacamole delegates the authentication to Keycloak via the "Implicit Fow". So I disabled the "Standard Flow".
In the UI if "Standard Flow" is unchecked, the field "Valid redirect URIs" is not displayed.

To resolved the problem, I did it like this in the UI : 
- check the "Standard Fow" box,
- Entering the "Valid redirect URIs",
- uncheck the "Standard Fow" box

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Display the "Valid redirect URIs" field when "Standard flow" is unchecked (mandatory for the "Implicit flow")

### Actual behavior

 "Valid redirect URIs" field is not displayed when "Standard flow" is unchecked

### How to Reproduce?

Uncheck "Standard flow"

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Client Details: show redirect URIs w/ implicit flow (#33120)

Fixes #33095

Signed-off-by: Stan Silvert <ssilvert@redhat.com>

The admin console is accessible and working fine.
We are able to connect to Nextcloud or Wordpress thanks to Keyloaker.

issue_title: Duplicate principals not allowed in keystore
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When building out a keystore to be used for mTLS client certificate verification, the truststores use a hashmap Map<X500Principal, X509Certificate>. 

Unfortunately when adding certificates with duplicate principal names, the resulting truststore does not have all the desired certificates. This prohibits the use of CA's with periods of overlapping validity. 




### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Certificates with duplicate principal names should be allowed.

### Actual behavior

When certificates with duplicate principal names are provided, only one of the certificates is used.

### How to Reproduce?

Add two certificate roots with the same principal name to the trust store. Try to authenticate with certificates signed by each, only one of the certificates will be valid.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow certificate with duplicate principals in truststore.

The previous implementation uses principal as a key for a hashmap storing one certificate per entry. To preserve lookups, the value is now a List of certificates.

Additional logic was added to build certification validation chains using signature verification rather than just principal.

Closes #33125

Signed-off-by: Matt Eaton <git@divinehawk.com>
